description_long: This weathered stone obelisk stands on a broad, rectangular base with a slightly sloped top and a thick, blocky plinth. The front face features faint, eroded inscriptions, suggesting it serves as a memorial or gravestone. The sides are relatively plain, but each has a scroll-like volute at the top, adding a subtle decorative touch. The stone surface is mottled with patches of moss and discoloration, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The base is partially embedded in earth and grass, with some visible chipping and wear at the corners. Overall, the monument exudes a sense of solemnity and historical significance, typical of older urban cemeteries or parks.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with faint inscriptions, scroll-like volutes at the top sides, and a broad, mossy base showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main inscription panel, sloped top, and mossy, chipped base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the scroll-like volute at the top and the thickness of the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned surface and the full width of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another scroll-like volute and visible weathering along the edge.
attribute_inscription: Faint, eroded inscription on the front face in uppercase Latin script; partially illegible due to weathering.
attribute_ornamentation: Scroll-like volutes at the top corners on both sides; otherwise minimal ornamentation.
attribute_base_design: Broad, rectangular base with a sloped upper section and thick plinth; corners are chipped and mossy, with some earth encroachment.
attribute_tip_design: Flat, slightly rounded tip with no additional decoration or unique features.
attribute_surrounding_features: Patches of grass and soil at the base; no other notable surrounding features visible.
